questions.text: At equilibrium, the rate of forward reaction is _____ the rate of backward reaction.
questions.explanation: This is the definition of dynamic equilibrium.

questions.text: The relation between Kp and Kc is:
questions.explanation: Δn is the change in number of moles of gaseous products and reactants.

questions.text: According to Le Chatelier’s Principle, increasing pressure on the system N₂(g) + 3H₂(g) ⇌ 2NH₃(g) will shift equilibrium to:
questions.explanation: Equilibrium shifts to the side with fewer moles of gas.

questions.text: A Bronsted-Lowry acid is a substance that:
The pH of a 0.01 M HCl solution is:
A Lewis base is a species that can:
questions.explanation: Example: NH₃ has a lone pair to donate.

questions.text: The ionic product of water (Kw) at 298 K is:
Which of the following is a buffer solution?
questions.explanation: A weak acid and its salt with a strong base form an acidic buffer.

questions.text: For a sparingly soluble salt AB₂, the solubility product Ksp is related to solubility S as:
If the reaction quotient Q > Kc, the reaction will proceed in:
questions.explanation: The system will try to reduce the concentration of products.

questions.text: Adding a catalyst to an equilibrium mixture:
questions.explanation: Catalyst only helps reach equilibrium faster.

questions.text: pH + pOH is always equal to:
Conjugate base of HSO₄⁻ is:
questions.explanation: Base formed after an acid donates H⁺.

questions.text: An aqueous solution with pH 9 is:
Which salt undergoes cationic hydrolysis?
questions.explanation: Salt of weak base and strong acid.

questions.text: Common ion effect _____ the solubility of a sparingly soluble salt.
questions.explanation: Presence of a common ion shifts equilibrium to left (precipitation).

questions.text: Inert gas addition at constant volume _____ the equilibrium.
questions.explanation: Total pressure increases but partial pressures remain same.

questions.text: Ostwald's Dilution Law is applicable to:
questions.explanation: Relates degree of dissociation to concentration.

questions.text: Henderson-Hasselbalch equation is used to calculate:
Degree of dissociation (α) increases with:
Value of K depends only on:
questions.explanation: It is a characteristic constant for a reaction at a specific T.

questions.text: Exothermic reactions are favoured by _____ temperature.
questions.explanation: Reducing heat shifts equilibrium to exothermic side.

questions.text: Which gas is used to maintain pH of blood?

Which indicator is used for titration of strong acid and strong base?
questions.explanation: Both show sharp color change at pH 7.

questions.text: Solubility of AgCl in NaCl solution is _____ than in pure water.
Active mass of a solid is taken as:
questions.explanation: Concentration of pure solids is constant.

questions.text: Kp is used for reactions involving:
questions.explanation: Partial pressures apply only to gases.

questions.explanation: It has a lone pair on N.
